title: Linguistics and Human Brain: A Perspective of Computational Neuroscience
abstract: arXiv:2602.08275v3 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Elucidating the language-brain relationship requires bridging the methodological gap between the abstract theoretical frameworks of linguistics and the empirical neural data of neuroscience. Serving as an interdisciplinary cornerstone, computational neuroscience formalizes the hierarchical and dynamic structures of language into testable neural models through modeling, simulation, and data analysis. This enables a computational dialogue between linguistic hypotheses and neural mechanisms. Recent advances in deep learning, particularly large language models (LLMs), have powerfully advanced this pursuit. Their high-dimensional representational spaces provide a novel scale for exploring the neural basis of linguistic processing, while the "model-brain alignment" framework offers a methodology to evaluate the biological plausibility of language-related theories.

translated_title: 语言学与人类大脑：计算神经科学的视角
translated_abstract: 摘要：阐明语言与大脑之间的关系，需要在语言学的抽象理论框架与神经科学的经验神经数据之间架起方法论的桥梁。作为跨学科的基石，计算神经科学通过建模、模拟和数据分析，将语言的层级性和动态结构形式化为可检验的神经模型，从而实现语言学假设与神经机制之间的计算对话。深度学习的最新进展，特别是大型语言模型（LLMs），极大地推动了这一探索。它们的高维表征空间为探索语言处理的神经基础提供了新的尺度，而“模型-大脑对齐”框架则提供了一种评估语言相关理论生物合理性的方法论。
tldr: 本文通过计算神经科学方法，利用大型语言模型和“模型-大脑对齐”框架，弥合语言学理论与神经数据之间的鸿沟，以揭示语言处理的神经基础。
en_tdlr: This paper bridges the gap between linguistic theories and neural data using computational neuroscience methods, leveraging large language models and a "model-brain alignment" framework to uncover the neural basis of language processing.
